title: Assessment of Clinical Criteria for Sepsis
country: United States (primary cohort); also Germany and multi-site US

sample_size.unit: encounters with suspected infection (primary UPMC cohort)
sample_size.source_quote: 148 907 encounters had suspected infection, most often presenting outside of the ICU (n = 133 139 [89%])

sofa_score_baseline.source_quote: SOFA near onset of suspected infection Mean (SD) 2.0 (2.7)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, weighted Charlson comorbidity score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: In the ICU, the predictive validity for hospital mortality using SOFA (AUROC = 0.74; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76) and LODS (AUROC = 0.75; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76; P = .20) were not statistically different but were statistically greater than that of SIRS (AUROC = 0.64; 95% CI, 0.62-0.66; P < .001 for either LODS or SOFA vs SIRS)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, weighted Charlson comorbidity score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: In the ICU, the predictive validity for hospital mortality using SOFA (AUROC = 0.74; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76) and LODS (AUROC = 0.75; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76; P = .20) were not statistically different but were statistically greater than that of SIRS (AUROC = 0.64; 95% CI, 0.62-0.66; P < .001 for either LODS or SOFA vs SIRS)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.sensitivity: 91% (95% CI, 89%-92%) among decedents with ≥2 SIRS criteria
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, weighted Charlson comorbidity score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: SOFA and LODS had greater statistical agreement with each other (α = 0.87; 95% CI, 0.87-0.88) but lower with SIRS ... In the ICU, the predictive validity for hospital mortality using SOFA (AUROC = 0.74; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76) and LODS (AUROC = 0.75; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76; P = .20) were not statistically different but were statistically greater than that of SIRS (AUROC = 0.64; 95% CI, 0.62-0.66; P < .001 for either LODS or SOFA vs SIRS)

mortality_predictors.timing: Up to 48 hours before to up to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, weighted Charlson comorbidity score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Results for a change in SOFA of 2 points or more were significantly greater compared with SIRS (AUROC = 0.70; 95% CI, 0.68-0.71; P < .001 vs SIRS criteria)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.sensitivity: 70% (95% CI, 69%-72%) of decedents had ≥2 qSOFA points (non-ICU)
mortality_predictors.specificity: 78% (95% CI, 78%-79%) of survivors had <2 qSOFA points (non-ICU)
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, weighted Charlson comorbidity score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: In the ICU, the predictive validity for hospital mortality of qSOFA above baseline risk (AUROC = 0.66; 95% CI, 0.64-0.68) was statistically greater than SIRS criteria (P = .01) but significantly less than SOFA (P < .001) ... Outside of the ICU ... The predictive validity of qSOFA was good for in-hospital mortality (AUROC = 0.81; 95% CI, 0.80-0.82), was not statistically different from LODS (P = .77) and was statistically greater than SOFA or change in SOFA score (P < .001 for both)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.sensitivity: 68% (95% CI, 66%-70%) of decedents had ≥2 SOFA points
mortality_predictors.specificity: 67% (95% CI, 66%-67%) of survivors had <2 SOFA points
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, weighted Charlson comorbidity score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The discrimination of hospital mortality using SOFA (AUROC = 0.79; 95% CI, 0.78-0.80), LODS (AUROC = 0.82; 95% CI, 0.81-0.83), or change in SOFA (AUROC = 0.79; 95% CI, 0.78-0.79) scores was significantly greater compared with SIRS criteria (AUROC = 0.76; 95% CI, 0.75-0.77; P < .01 for all)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, weighted Charlson comorbidity score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The discrimination of hospital mortality using SOFA (AUROC = 0.79; 95% CI, 0.78-0.80), LODS (AUROC = 0.82; 95% CI, 0.81-0.83), or change in SOFA (AUROC = 0.79; 95% CI, 0.78-0.79) scores was significantly greater compared with SIRS criteria (AUROC = 0.76; 95% CI, 0.75-0.77; P < .01 for all)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.sensitivity: 64% (95% CI, 62%-67%) of decedents had ≥2 SIRS criteria
mortality_predictors.specificity: 65% (95% CI, 64%-65%) of survivors had <2 SIRS criteria
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, weighted Charlson comorbidity score
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The discrimination of hospital mortality using SOFA (AUROC = 0.79; 95% CI, 0.78-0.80), LODS (AUROC = 0.82; 95% CI, 0.81-0.83), or change in SOFA (AUROC = 0.79; 95% CI, 0.78-0.79) scores was significantly greater compared with SIRS criteria (AUROC = 0.76; 95% CI, 0.75-0.77; P < .01 for all)

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: qSOFA score (external validation - KCEMS community infection)
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Among encounters with community infection (KCEMS) or hospital-acquired infection (ALERTS), qSOFA had consistent predictive validity (AUROC = 0.71 and 0.75, respectively)

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: qSOFA score (external validation - ALERTS hospital-acquired infection)
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Among encounters with community infection (KCEMS) or hospital-acquired infection (ALERTS), qSOFA had consistent predictive validity (AUROC = 0.71 and 0.75, respectively)
Results were similar in the VA data set (AUROC = 0.78), in which no GCS data were available.

the post hoc addition of serum lactate levels of 2.0 mmol/L (18 mg/dL) or more to qSOFA (revised to a 4-point score with 1 added point for elevated serum lactate level) statistically changed the predictive validity of qSOFA (AUROC with lactate = 0.80; 95% CI, 0.79-0.81 vs AUROC without lactate = 0.79; 95% CI, 0.78-0.80; P < .001)
